2603.19415.title: Scalable Prompt Routing via Fine-Grained Latent Task Discovery
2603.19415.paper_summary: Prompt routing dynamically selects the most appropriate large language model from a pool of candidates for each query, optimizing performance while managing costs. As model pools scale to include dozens of frontier models with narrow performance gaps, existing approaches face significant challenges: manually defined task taxonomies cannot capture fine-grained capability distinctions, while monolithic routers struggle to differentiate subtle differences across diverse tasks. We propose a two-stage routing architecture that addresses these limitations through automated fine-grained task discovery and task-aware quality estimation. Our first stage employs graph-based clustering to discover latent task types and trains a classifier to assign prompts to discovered tasks. The second stage uses a mixture-of-experts architecture with task-specific prediction heads for specialized quality estimates. At inference, we aggregate predictions from both stages to balance task-level stability with prompt-specific adaptability. Evaluated on 10 benchmarks with 11 frontier models, our method consistently outperforms existing baselines and surpasses the strongest individual model while incurring less than half its cost.
2603.19415.paper_summary_zh: 提示路由動態選擇最合適的大型語言模型，從候選池中為每個查詢進行優化性能並管理成本。隨著模型池的擴展，包含數十個前沿模型且性能差距狹窄，現有方法面臨重大挑戰：手動定義的任務分類無法捕捉細微的能力區別，而單一路由器在不同任務之間難以區分微妙的差異。我們提出了一種兩階段路由架構，通過自動化的細粒度任務發現和任務感知的質量評估來解決這些限制。我們的第一階段採用基於圖的聚類來發現潛在的任務類型，並訓練分類器將提示分配給發現的任務。第二階段使用專門的質量預測頭的專家混合架構，以獲得專門的質量評估。在推理時，我們從兩個階段聚合預測，以平衡任務級別的穩定性與提示特定的適應性。在10個基準測試中評估，使用11個前沿模型，我們的方法始終超越現有基準，並超越最強的單一模型，同時成本不到其一半。
2603.19415.author: Yunyi Zhang et.al.
2603.19415.authors: Yunyi Zhang, Soji Adeshina, Patrick Guan, Ashwin Ganesh, Zhen Han, Vassilis N. Ioannidis, Huzefa Rangwala, George Karypis
